spec_match.rationale: Parses the MIME header from a dropped file, lists each parameter explicitly with a 'preserved in v146' verdict, builds before-vs-after data URLs that strip vs keep parameters, and renders the result across ``, ``, and `

interactive.rationale: Drag-and-drop file input, three presets (PDF/CSV/JPEG), a parameters table, a before/after URL toggle, and a render grid spanning multiple HTML embedding surfaces — the richest interactive surface.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises file drop + FileReader, parameters table with v146-vs-pre-v146, before/after toggle, three presets, and a render test across img / a-download / iframe — page delivers all of those.

edge_cases.rationale: Three structured presets (`application/pdf; version=1.4`, `text/csv; charset=utf-8; header=present`, `image/jpeg; quality=80`) are excellent — but the file-drop branch shows 'No MIME parameters in this file type' for most real-world files, because OS-supplied MIME strings rarely include parameters. Authors will hit that empty-table case as the default.

references.rationale: The page has no `see also` section at all. No link to the chromestatus feature page, no link to the Fetch spec data-URL section, no link to MDN. This breaks the self-heal invariant in CLAUDE.md #3.

design_system.rationale: Variables-driven for most styles, but `badge-ok` / `badge-warn` classes likely use raw hex backgrounds (need to verify). Inline `style="color:var(--text-muted); ..."` is fine. Bigger concern: file input and several panels use inline `style="display:none"` toggling rather than the design system's class-based hidden pattern — minor inconsistency.

openQuestions.title: Add the references section
openQuestions.detail: The page has no see-also block, breaking the self-heal invariant. Add `https://chromestatus.com/feature/4874471565557760`, the Fetch spec data-URL section, and links to sibling concepts.

openQuestions.title: Pre-populate parameters for typical OS-supplied MIME strings
openQuestions.detail: When the visitor drops a real file from disk, the OS-supplied MIME string almost never includes parameters, so the parameters table immediately renders 'No MIME parameters in this file type'. Add a 'inject example parameters' affordance (or auto-suggest charset for text/* and version for application/pdf) so the headline case actually populates.

openQuestions.title: Show the corruption that the spec change prevents
openQuestions.detail: The page demonstrates that v146 preserves parameters, but doesn't show what corrupts when they're stripped. Add an ISO-8859-1 CSV preset where the before-URL renders mojibake and the after-URL renders correctly in the iframe column.

openQuestions.title: Verify badge palette uses CSS variables
openQuestions.detail: `.badge-ok` / `.badge-warn` (and `.badge-muted`) appear to use raw hex backgrounds in the inline `<style>` block — audit and convert to variables (CLAUDE.md #5).

summary: Richest interactive surface in the feature folder, but it ships without a see-also block (breaking the self-heal invariant) and shows an empty parameters table for the headline case (a dropped file with OS-supplied MIME). Add references, pre-populate parameters for plain-MIME drops, and ship a corruption-when-stripped demo.
